frameworks.name: connectome-based large-scale cortex model
frameworks.one_line: macaque-connectome network model with multiple neuron types and area-specific receptor density

frameworks.name: distributed persistent activity / attractor working memory
frameworks.one_line: persistent activity arises across many areas, modulated by a dopamine gradient

frameworks.name: activity-silent working memory
frameworks.one_line: memory held via short-term facilitation of inter-areal connections, no spiking trace

core_question: How does a macroscopic dopamine gradient across the primate cortical hierarchy shape distributed working memory?
key_assumptions: dopamine receptor density varies systematically along the cortical hierarchy
large-scale dynamics can be captured by a connectome-constrained spiking model
dendrite-targeting interneurons mediate dopaminergic gating of inputs
manipulations: cortical dopamine level in the model
dopamine receptor density gradient incorporated from autoradiography
presence vs absence of distractor (irrelevant) stimuli
key_findings: dopamine receptor density per neuron forms a macroscopic gradient along the macaque cortical hierarchy
model reproduces an inverted U-shaped dependence of working memory on dopamine
model captures spatial patterns of persistent activity matching over 90 experimental studies
dopamine filters irrelevant stimuli by enhancing inhibition from dendrite-targeting interneurons
facilitation of inter-areal connections can implement an activity-silent memory trace
interpretations: a cortex-wide dopamine gradient gates access to distributed working memory
adjusting cortical dopamine switches the network between activity-silent and distributed persistent memory states
